articles.title: 2026 NBA Playoff Bracket: First‑Round Matchups Begin
articles.original_title: 2026 NBA playoff bracket: Matchups, schedule with first round underway
articles.summary: The 2026 NBA playoffs have started, featuring eight first‑round series across the league. The bracket outlines each matchup and the schedule for the opening round, setting the stage for the postseason.
key_points: Eight first‑round series are underway.
CBS Sports provides the full bracket and game dates.
All teams are competing for a path to the conference semifinals.

articles.title: Tatum Scores 25 as Celtics Take 2‑1 Lead Over 76ers
articles.original_title: Tatum shines with 25 points as Celtics take 2-1 series lead over 76ers
articles.summary: Jayson Tatum returned from injury to drop 25 points, matching Jaylen Brown’s 25, as Boston defeated Philadelphia 108‑100 to lead the Eastern Conference first‑round series 2‑1. Game 4 is scheduled for Sunday in Philadelphia.
key_points: Tatum and Brown each contributed 25 points.
Celtics win 108‑100, moving ahead 2‑1 in the series.
Game 4 will be played Sunday in Philadelphia.

articles.title: Spurs Beat Trail Blazers 120‑108 Without Wembanyama, Take 2‑1 Series Lead
articles.original_title: Spurs beat Trail Blazers 120-108 without Wembanyama to take 2-1 series lead
articles.summary: Despite missing Victor Wembanyama, the San Antonio Spurs rallied behind Stephon Castle’s 33 points and Dylan Harper’s 27 points and 10 rebounds to defeat Portland 120‑108, gaining a 2‑1 advantage in the series.
key_points: Stephon Castle led with 33 points.
Dylan Harper added 27 points and a double‑double.
Spurs overcame a 15‑point third‑quarter deficit to win.

articles.title: LeBron James Delivers ‘Everything’ in Lakers’ Game 3 Win Over Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron James promised the Lakers 'everything' and delivered in spectacular fashion in Game 3 win over Rockets
articles.summary: At age 41, LeBron James produced another standout playoff performance, helping the Los Angeles Lakers secure a decisive Game 3 victory against the Houston Rockets. The win highlighted James’s continued impact in high‑stakes games.
key_points: LeBron, 41, posted a marquee playoff performance.
Lakers clinched Game 3 against the Rockets.
James’s effort was described as delivering “everything.”

articles.title: Why the Rockets Fell Short in Game 3 vs. Lakers
articles.original_title: So how did the Rockets blow Game 3 against the Lakers?
articles.summary: Houston outshot the Lakers and secured more offensive rebounds, but recurring season‑long issues—such as turnovers and defensive lapses—prevented them from converting those advantages into a win in Game 3.
key_points: Rockets took more shots and grabbed more offensive boards.
Persistent problems like turnovers and poor defense persisted.
These issues ultimately led to the loss against the Lakers.

articles.title: Rockets Lose 6‑Point Lead in Final Seconds, Fall to Lakers in OT
articles.original_title: A 6-point lead, 30 seconds left, and a Rockets unraveling that flipped Game 3
articles.summary: Houston led Los Angeles by six with under 30 seconds remaining, but a late collapse allowed the Lakers to force overtime and win 112‑108. The loss puts the Rockets on the brink of elimination in the first round of the Western Conference playoffs.
key_points: Rockets held a six‑point advantage with 30 seconds left before the Lakers erased the lead.
Los Angeles forced overtime and secured a 112‑108 victory.
The defeat leaves Houston facing imminent elimination in the series.

articles.title: Portland’s Deni Avdija Chips Tooth in Incident with Spurs’ De'Aaron Fox
articles.original_title: Blazers star Deni Avdija has tooth chipped by Spurs' De'Aaron Fox, then gets called for a foul
articles.summary: During a game, Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija suffered a chipped tooth after a collision with San Antonio Spurs guard De'Aaron Fox and was subsequently assessed a foul. The injury will require dental treatment.
key_points: Avdija’s tooth was chipped in a play involving De'Aaron Fox.
He was called for a personal foul following the incident.
Medical attention, including a dentist visit, is expected.

articles.title: Immanuel Quickley Won’t Face Cavaliers in First‑Round Playoffs
articles.original_title: Cavs won’t face Immanuel Quickley in the playoffs
articles.summary: Toronto Raptors point guard Immanuel Quickley will not be available to play against the Cleveland Cavaliers in the opening round of the postseason. The decision removes him from the series lineup.
key_points: Quickley is ruled out for the Raptors' first‑round matchup with Cleveland.
His absence affects Toronto’s backcourt rotation.
The Cavaliers will not have to contend with Quickley’s playmaking.

articles.title: New 76ers Rookies Jonathan Greenard and Makai Lemon Experience Playoff Atmosphere
articles.original_title: Newest Eagles Jonathan Greenard, Makai Lemon take in Sixers playoff game
articles.summary: Recent Philadelphia 76ers additions Jonathan Greenard and Makai Lemon attended the team’s playoff game, getting a firsthand look at the city’s passionate basketball culture. Their presence highlights the organization’s integration of new talent during the postseason.
key_points: Greenard and Lemon attended the Sixers’ playoff game on Friday night.
The experience gave them exposure to Philadelphia’s intense fan environment.
Their attendance underscores the team’s effort to involve recent acquisitions in the playoff run.

articles.title: Jayson Tatum Delivers Game‑Winning 3‑Pointer in Celtics’ Game 3 Victory
articles.original_title: Jayson Tatum says he's not at his best yet. He sure looked the part for Boston in a Game 3 win
articles.summary: Boston Celtics star Jayson Tatum hit a decisive three‑pointer late in Game 3, silencing the Philadelphia 76ers and securing the win. Despite saying he is not yet at his best, the clutch shot highlighted his impact in the series.
key_points: Tatum’s late three‑pointer proved decisive in the Celtics’ Game 3 win.
He acknowledged he is still working toward his peak performance.
The shot halted a late 76ers rally and shifted momentum in Boston’s favor.

articles.title: LeBron James lands improbable off‑the‑glass buzzer‑beater
articles.original_title: LeBron James hits an improbable shot off the glass - ESPN
articles.summary: In the closing moments of the Lakers’ Game 3 against the Houston Rockets, LeBron James hit a highly unlikely shot off the glass to force overtime. The play sparked a surge that helped Los Angeles secure a 3‑0 series lead.
key_points: LeBron’s shot came with seconds left in regulation and was off a missed attempt that bounced off the glass.
The basket sent the game to overtime, where the Lakers ultimately prevailed.
The win gave the Lakers a commanding 3‑0 advantage in the playoff series.

articles.title: NBA community reacts to LeBron’s Game 3 performance
articles.original_title: NBA World Reacts To LeBron James’ Performance In Lakers-Rockets Game - heavy.com
articles.summary: Fans, analysts, and former players shared their thoughts on LeBron James’ clutch showing in the Lakers’ overtime win over the Rockets. The consensus highlighted his leadership and ability to deliver in high‑pressure moments.
key_points: Social media buzz praised LeBron’s shot and overall impact on the game.
Commentators noted his veteran poise and scoring efficiency despite missing teammates.
Many viewed the performance as a reminder of his playoff pedigree.

articles.title: LeBron’s clutch shot pushes Lakers to OT and 3‑0 lead
articles.original_title: WATCH: LeBron James' clutch shot sends Lakers to OT, 3-0 lead vs. Rockets - Yahoo Sports Canada
articles.summary: A video recap captured LeBron James’ decisive buzzer‑beater that sent the Lakers‑Rockets Game 3 into overtime, extending Los Angeles’ series lead to 3‑0. The play was highlighted as a pivotal moment in the series.
key_points: The shot occurred in the final seconds of regulation, forcing overtime.
Lakers won the overtime period, sealing a 3‑0 series advantage.
The clip quickly went viral, emphasizing LeBron’s clutch reputation.

articles.title: LeBron James fills stat sheet in Lakers’ victory
articles.original_title: Lakers' LeBron James: Stuffs stat sheet in win - CBS Sports
articles.summary: LeBron James posted a near‑triple‑double performance in the Lakers’ win over the Rockets, contributing across points, rebounds, assists, and defensive stats. His all‑around effort was a key factor in the team’s success.
key_points: LeBron recorded double‑digit numbers in points, rebounds, assists, steals, and blocks.
His versatile play helped offset the absence of key Rockets players.
The stat‑filled line underscored his impact beyond scoring.

articles.title: LeBron dazzles in OT win as Rockets miss Luka, KD, Reaves
articles.original_title: LeBron James Wows Fans in Lakers OT Win vs. Rockets in Game 3 with Luka, KD, Reaves Out - Bleacher Report
articles.summary: With Luka Dončić, Kevin Durant and Austin Reaves out, LeBron James stepped up in Game 3, leading the Lakers to an overtime victory over the Rockets. His performance kept Los Angeles on track for a series sweep.
key_points: The Rockets were shorthanded, missing three of their top contributors.
LeBron’s scoring and playmaking carried the Lakers through overtime.
The win positioned the Lakers just one game away from advancing.
